mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a strengthening economic foundation, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a primary NBER business cycle dating indicator.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in PAYEMS exhibits a rolling 36-month correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns of less than 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, any increase, however small, signals a major policy regime shift towards tightening. This increases the equity risk premium and negatively impacts valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.10% from a level below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS rate is 0.11, near the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts a positive return over the 60 trading days following the first two rate increases off the zero lower bound.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of accelerating retail sales and rising inflation creates fears of an overheating economy, forcing the central bank to tighten policy. This joint signal of strong demand and price pressure is negative for forward equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When starting from an elevated level, a decrease in the unemployment rate is a powerful confirmation of a durable economic recovery, reducing uncertainty and boosting investor sentiment, leading to positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 9.7%, well above historical norms.
failure_condition: The signal's information coefficient (IC) is not statistically significantly higher when UNRATE > 7% compared to when UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Momentum in industrial production is a direct, real-time indicator of output and corporate activity. Positive momentum translates into higher expected revenues and earnings, supporting higher equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Conference Board Coincident Economic Index.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts is a leading indicator of a severe contraction in a key cyclical sector, signaling falling consumer confidence and investment, which negatively impacts broad market returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average falls by > 15% year-over-year
evidence_refs: fact:The collapse in HOUST was a primary leading indicator for the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of a low, stable federal funds rate and a high unemployment rate signals that monetary policy will remain accommodative for an extended period, supporting risk assets through low discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:This describes the post-GFC policy environment.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when the trigger condition is met is not statistically greater than the unconditional average return (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A strong month-over-month increase in retail sales is a high-frequency indicator of consumer health, leading to immediate positive revisions of GDP and earnings growth expectations by market participants.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a top-decile monthly RSAFS release is not significantly different from zero.
